name: github-multi-repo
description: Multi-repository coordination, synchronization, and architecture management with AI swarm orchestration
capabilities: Coordinate changes across multiple GitHub repositories using AI swarm orchestration
Synchronize package versions and dependencies across repositories
Analyze and optimize repository architecture with automated templates
Create standardized project templates for new repositories
Deploy security patches and dependency updates across all repositories
Generate cross-repository dependency graphs and health reports
author: Claude Flow Team
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill (SKILL.md) with no executable code. All security patterns detected are false positives - the file contains documentation examples showing how to use external tools (gh CLI, claude-flow MCP). These examples require explicit user invocation and operate only on repositories the user has access to via authenticated gh CLI. The pre-computed skill-report.json correctly shows risk_level: 'safe' with zero findings.
valueStatement: Managing multiple GitHub repositories requires coordinating changes, dependencies, and releases across repositories. This skill uses AI swarm orchestration to synchronize packages, standardize architecture, and coordinate cross-repository operations automatically.
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) and claude-flow MCP server installation
Cannot access private repositories without proper gh authentication
Operates on repositories you have explicit access to via gh CLI
Swarm operations depend on available MCP server resources

useCases.title: Synchronize microservices
useCases.description: Keep shared libraries and API contracts consistent across all microservices in your organization

useCases.title: Coordinate cross-repo releases
useCases.description: Deploy coordinated releases across multiple repositories with synchronized version bumps

useCases.title: Deploy security patches
useCases.description: Apply security updates to all repositories and create automated pull requests

promptTemplates.title: Initialize coordination
promptTemplates.prompt: Use github-multi-repo to initialize coordination for repositories: org/frontend, org/backend, org/shared with hierarchical topology

promptTemplates.title: Sync packages
promptTemplates.prompt: Synchronize package versions for claude-code-flow and ruv-swarm. Align versions and update documentation across both repositories

promptTemplates.title: Standardize structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze repository structure and create standardized templates for new Claude Code projects in my organization

promptTemplates.title: Deploy security updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Scan all my organization repositories for vulnerabilities, apply npm audit fixes, and create pull requests with security updates

bestPractices: Use hierarchical topology for clear ownership and mesh for peer-to-peer coordination
Create tracking issues before organization-wide changes to monitor progress
Test changes in a single repository before propagating across all repositories
antiPatterns: Running swarm operations without specifying repository boundaries
Applying strong consistency sync for documentation changes
Ignoring failed tests in individual repositories before propagating changes

faq.question: What permissions does this skill need?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI authentication for repos you access. No additional permissions beyond gh CLI.

faq.question: How many repositories can this skill coordinate?
faq.answer: Scales to hundreds of repositories. Adjust maxAgents based on system resources.

faq.question: Can I use this with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, if gh CLI is authenticated and you have access to those repositories.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: All operations are explicit via gh CLI. No hidden data collection or external calls.

faq.question: What sync strategies are available?
faq.answer: Eventual consistency for documentation, strong consistency for security updates, hybrid for mixed workloads.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual coordination?
faq.answer: Automates repetitive tasks across repos. Reduces errors and ensures consistency compared to manual approaches.
